title: Find Digital Root of a Number
description:
The digital root of a number is the recursive sum of all its digits. Keep summing the digits until you get a single digit.

For example: 38 → 3+8=11 → 1+1=2
hints: You can solve this iteratively by repeatedly summing digits.
There's a mathematical formula: 1 + ((n-1) % 9) for n > 0.
The digital root is related to the number modulo 9.
